content.problemStatement:
With the help of Sheska, Edward now has the research notes of Dr. Marcho. To prevent the secret of Philosopher stone falling into the wrong hands, Marcho has encoded the secret in the form of a list of triplets of numbers. Edward remembers that Dr. Marcho gave him a hint while he was leaving, "Sometimes you should try transmutation rectangles instead of circles, maybe they are better for making philosopher stones". Edward first thought that this was a joke, but he has understood the hint now. He interprets each triplet as the length, breadth, and color of a rectangle centered at the origin. Given below is his interpretation to decode the numbers.

There are NNN **colored** rectangles numbered from 111 to NNN, with the iii-th rectangle having length LiL_{i}Li​, breadth BiB_{i}Bi​ and color CiC_{i}Ci​. Each rectangle is **centered at the origin**. That is, if for any rectangle, we draw both diagonals, the diagonals shall intersect at the origin. For example, a rectangle having length 10 and breadth 2 would have its 4 corners as (5, 1), (5, -1), (-5, 1) and (-5, -1).

The rectangles are placed one over the other. Specifically, the rectangles are placed in the order in which they appear in the input, overlapping the previous rectangles. The rectangle iii is partially hidden by the rectangle jjj if jjj > iii. That is, only the colour of the top rectangle is visible in the overlapping region.

Edward believes that the ratio of the area of each color in the final figure is pointing to some ratio of ingredients for making the philosopher stone.

Can you find out how many unit squares of each colour are visible in the final figure?

Input:
The first line contains TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
For each test case, the first line consists of NNN, denoting the number of rectangles and CCC  denoting the number of colours.
For each test case, NNN lines follow, the iii-th of which contains three integers LiL_{i}Li​, BiB_{i}Bi​ and CiC_{i}Ci​ describing length, breadth and color of the iii-th rectangle respectively.
Output:For each test case, output a single line containing CCC space-separated integers denoting the number of unit squares of each color 111 to CCC visible in the final figure.
constraints: LiL_{i}Li​ and BiB_{i}Bi​ are even for all 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N
Sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 10610^{6}106
In the above figure, Green represents color 111, Blue represents color 222, Yellow represents color 333,  and Red color represents color 444.
